terminalCommandInputTooltip: Argv line: ffmpeg, ffprobe, or yt-dlp. Enter runs; arrow keys cycle hints and history.
terminalCommandToolbarAria: Command line, insert file, and run
terminalDropdownFilterAria: Filter for the full terminal hint list
terminalDropdownFilterPlaceholder: Search by token, summary, or fullLine
terminalDropdownInsertLabel: Insert a hint from the full list
terminalHintDocLinkAria: Open documentation for this hint in the browser
terminalHintsInsertListAria: Hints: insert a token or full line into the command
terminalHintsSearchHint: Narrows the list on the right. Click a row to insert a token or the full command (example at the bottom of the card). Search by words like “track”, “volume”, “trim”, not “v:0”.
terminalHistoryEmpty: No commands in this session yet.
terminalHistoryHint: Empty until the first command; each output line has a hover “Copy” button (that line only).
terminalIntroKnowledgeNavAria: Navigation: open terminal hints article in the knowledge base
terminalIntroLead: Only the ffmpeg, ffprobe, and yt-dlp prefixes are allowed. The command is parsed as argv, runs in the main process without a shell, and PATH is extended with the selected engine folder. In argv you can use the placeholder
terminalIntroTailTemplate: — it expands to the current editor preview path (only when a file is already open via the dialog or DnD). While typing, the input shows compact IntelliSense: Up/Down, Home/End, PgUp/PgDn (step {pageStep}), Shift+Tab moves the selection up, Tab and Enter insert the active suggestion (up to {maxInline} items from the same catalog as the sidebar). The full catalog is in the right-hand panel: use “Filter hints”, click a row (when a hint has a full command line, it replaces the whole line). Each line in the output log has a “Copy” hover button (copies exactly that line).
terminalMainSplitHint: Left: command log and output; right: filterable hints catalog.
terminalPanelSectionAria: Terminal workspace: input, output log, and hints
terminalPreviewFileButtonAria: Insert current preview file path into the command
terminalPreviewFileTooltipNeedFile: Open a file in the editor first
terminalRunButtonTooltip: Run the command line (Enter). Pick hints on the right by words, not by stream index v:0.
